title: Migrate code-review-graph from programs.claude-code.mcpServers (old) to programs.mcp.servers (new)
description:
code-review-graph.nix:71 is the last consumer of programs.claude-code.mcpServers after commit e09499f unified MCP config via programs.mcp.servers. Migration was missed because the file is commented out in default.nix:57.

Goal: move the mcpServers entry to programs.mcp.servers."code-review-graph" so opencode also picks it up. Coordinates with the agents.tools refactor (this entry becomes agents.tools.codeReviewGraph.mcpServers."code-review-graph").
acceptance_criteria: No references to programs.claude-code.mcpServers in workstation/agents/. code-review-graph appears in programs.mcp.servers when enable=true.

title: Refactor: mkMcpServer helper + extract package builds to packages/
description:
mcp-servers.nix has 7 server entries built 7 slightly-different ways. github-mcp and kagi hand-roll shell wrappers to inject sops secrets. caveman-shrink is wrapped inline at 5 callsites. kagimcp's 25-line Python build sits inside the registration file.

Goal: introduce mkMcpServer { name, command, args ? [], secretEnvFiles ? {}, shrink ? false } and mkEmacsStdioServer { name, initFunction, stopFunction }. Move kagimcp build to packages/kagimcp.nix; move chrome-devtools-mcp build (currently default.nix:11-28) to packages/chrome-devtools-mcp.nix. mcp-servers.nix becomes a registration file.
acceptance_criteria: No inline shell-script wrappers in mcp-servers.nix. No inline package builds. All 7 servers go through helper. github-mcp + kagi still receive their sops secrets. caveman-shrink wrapping preserved for the 5 currently-shrunk servers.

title: Refactor: rename agents docs to {both,claudeOnly,opencodeOnly} + fix AGENTS.md union
description:
Current naming is misleading: extraClaudeDocs means 'both', extraOpencodeDocs means 'opencode-only', extraClaudeOnlyDocs means 'claude-only'. Worse, ~/.agents/AGENTS.md (combined) excludes extraOpencodeDocs despite naming.

Goal: rename top-level options to agents.docs.{both, claudeOnly, opencodeOnly} (lists). Tool submodule's docs field appends to these. AGENTS.md becomes a true union (both + claudeOnly + opencodeOnly). claude-code context = both + claudeOnly. opencode context = both + opencodeOnly.

Touches: default.nix (options + fold), caveman.nix (extraOpencodeDocs -> docs.opencodeOnly), beads.nix (same), rtk.nix (extraClaudeDocs -> docs.both), subagents.nix (folded into default.nix per Candidate A).
acceptance_criteria: No references to extraClaudeDocs/extraOpencodeDocs/extraClaudeOnlyDocs in agents/. ~/.agents/AGENTS.md contains content from all three streams.

title: Refactor: introduce agents.tools.<name> submodule + central fold
description:
Workstation/agents/ tool integrations (caveman, rtk, beads, code-review-graph) lack a shared shape. code-review-graph is enabled by uncommenting default.nix:57; no module gates on enable; chrome-devtools-mcp build sits in default.nix while its registration is in mcp-servers.nix.

Goal: introduce typed submodule agents.tools.<name> with fields { enable (default true), packages, mcpServers, hooks, docs.{both,claudeOnly,opencodeOnly}, skills, activation }. default.nix filters by enable and folds active tools into programs.{claude-code,opencode,mcp}, home.packages, agents.extraSkillPaths, home.activation.

Edge cases (caveman hooksDir/statusLine, security-guidance enabledPlugins) bypass via direct programs.claude-code.settings.* gated on cfg.enable.

Pass-throughs (security-guidance.nix, subagents.nix) collapse into default.nix as inline declarations.
acceptance_criteria: agents.tools.caveman.enable = false; disables caveman entirely (no hooks, no docs, no packages). nix flake check passes. home-manager build evaluates. claude-code starts with all tools active when defaults preserved.

title: Migrate agent config to programs.claude-code / programs.opencode module options
description: Replace home.file/xdg.configFile writes in workstation/agents/{default,skills,caveman}.nix with typed module options (context, skills, hooksDir, settings). Enable programs.opencode. Verify with nix build .#packages.x86_64-linux.default.
